title: Vehicle sensor systems, components and methods
abstract: A vehicle includes a vehicle controller communicating over a first communication system with actuators for changing the state of vehicle systems. A signal module communicates with the vehicle controller via the first communication system. One or more signal sources communicate with the signal module via a second communication system, and transmit signals to the signal module. Based on one or more signals and one or more user inputs, the signal module generates and transmits control signals to the vehicle controller. The vehicle controller actuates the actuator based on the control signal.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A signal module of a vehicle control system, the signal module comprising: a signal receiving portion constructed and arranged to receive a signal from a signal generator; a user option storage configured to retain a value for a user option; one or more processors coupled to memory, wherein the one or more processors are programed to: generate a control signal based on the signal received from the signal generator and the value for the user option; and a signal transmitting portion coupled to the one or more processors, the signal transmitting portion constructed and arranged to transmit the control signal to a vehicle controller. 2. The signal module according to claim 1, wherein the one or more processors coupled to memory comprise a first processor and a first memory and wherein the vehicle controller and the signal module both use the first processor and the first memory. 3. The signal module according to claim 1, wherein the one or more processors coupled to memory comprise a first processor coupled to a first memory and a second processor coupled to a second memory and wherein the vehicle controller uses the first processor and the first memory and wherein the signal module uses the second processor and the second memory. 4. The signal module according to claim 1, wherein: the vehicle control system further comprises a first communication system and the signal module further comprises a second communication system; and wherein the signal transmitting portion communicates with the first communication system and the signal receiving portion communicates with the second communication system. 5. The signal module according to claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further programed to cause the signal module to generate the control signal with instructions for the vehicle controller to alter a vehicle function in a first manner based on (i) a first value for the user option and (ii) the signal received from the signal generator and to cause the signal module to generate a second control signal with instructions for the vehicle controller to alter the vehicle function in a second manner based on (i) a second value for the user option and (ii) the signal received from the signal generator. 6. The signal module according to claim 5, wherein the vehicle function is a forward speed limit. 7. The signal module according to claim 6, wherein the signal received from the signal generator contains information relating to a distance of an object in front of a vehicle. 8. The signal module according to claim 5, wherein the vehicle function is a reverse speed limit. 9. The signal module according to claim 8, wherein the signal received from the signal generator contains information relating to a distance of an object behind a vehicle. 10. The signal module according to claim 5, wherein the vehicle function is a hold on a stop state. 11. The signal module according to claim 5, wherein: the signal receiving portion is further constructed and arranged to receive a second signal from a second signal generator; the user option storage is further configured to retain a value for a second user option, wherein the one or more processors are further programed to generate a second control signal based on the second signal received from the second signal generator and the value for the second user option. 12. The signal module according to claim 11, wherein the vehicle function is a hold on a stop state; and wherein the one or more processors are further programed to cause the signal module to generate a second control signal with instructions for the vehicle controller to alter a second vehicle function based on (i) the value for the second user option and (ii) the second signal received from the second signal generator, wherein the second vehicle function is an override of the hold on a stop state. 13. A vehicle comprising: a vehicle system; an electronically operated actuator operatively connected to the vehicle system for altering the vehicle system: 

 a vehicle controller communicating with the electronically operated actuator, wherein the vehicle controller comprises one or more processors coupled to memory, where the one or more processors are programmed to operate the electronically operated actuator; a signal module communicating with the vehicle controller, where the signal module causes the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator, in a first manner based on (i) a first user option and (ii) a signal and to cause the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator in a second manner based on (i) a second user option and (ii) the signal; and a signal generator communicating with the signal module, where the signal generator transmits the signal to the signal module. 14. The vehicle according to claim 13, wherein the signal module comprises the one or more processors coupled to memory. 15. The vehicle according to claim 13, wherein the signal module comprises one or more second processors coupled to a second memory. 16. The vehicle according to claim 13, further comprising: a first communication system for communication between the vehicle controller, the electronically operated actuator, and the signal module; and a second communication system for communication between the signal generator and the signal module. 17. The vehicle according to claim 16, further comprising: a second signal generator communicating with the signal module via the second electrical communication system, wherein the second signal generator is configured to transmit a second signal to the signal module; wherein the signal module causes the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator in a third manner based on (i) a third user option and (ii) the second signal and to cause the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator in a fourth manner based on (i) a fourth user option and (ii) the second signal. 18. The vehicle according to claim 17, further comprising: a second vehicle system; a second electronically operated actuator operatively connected to the second vehicle system for altering the second vehicle system, wherein the second electronically operated actuator communicates with the vehicle controller via the first communication system; wherein the signal module causes the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator and the second electronically operated actuator in a fifth manner based on (i) the first user option, (ii) the signal, and (iii) the second signal and to cause the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator and the second electronically operated actuator in a sixth manner based on (i) the second user option, (ii) the signal, and (ii) the second signal. 19. A method of operating a vehicle system with a vehicle control system, comprising: 

 receiving, with a signal module comprising one or more processors coupled to memory, a signal from a signal generator in communication with the signal module, where the signal generator transmits the signal to the signal module based on an activity state of the signal generator; receiving, with a vehicle controller comprising one or more processors coupled to memory, a command from the signal module to operate an electronically operated actuator operatively connected to the vehicle system in a first manner based on (i) a first user option and (ii) a signal and to cause the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator in a second manner based on (i) a second user option and (ii) the signal; thereby operating the vehicle system with the vehicle control system; and operating, with the vehicle controller, the electronically operated actuator. 20. The method according to claim 18, wherein the signal module and the vehicle controller operate on the same processor and the same memory. 21. The method according to claim 18, wherein the signal module and the vehicle controller operate on a different processor and a different memory. 22. The method according to claim 18, wherein the vehicle controller, the electronically operated actuator, and the signal module are in communication on a first communication system, and wherein the signal generator and the signal module are in communication on a second communication system. 23. The method according to claim 21, further comprising: receiving, with the signal module, a signal from a second signal generator in communication with the signal module, where the second signal generator transmits the signal to the signal module based on an activity state of the second signal generator; receiving, with the vehicle controller, a command from the signal module to operate the electronically operated actuator in a third manner based on (i) a third user option and (ii) the second signal and to cause the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator in a fourth manner based on (i) a fourth user option and (ii) the second signal; and operating, with the vehicle controller, the electronically operated actuator. 24. The method according to claim 23, further comprising: receiving, with the signal module, a signal from a signal generator in communication with the signal module, where the signal generator transmits the signal to the signal module based on an activity state of the signal generator; receiving, with the vehicle controller a command from the signal module to operate a second electronically operated actuator operatively connected to a second vehicle system, in a fifth manner based on (i) the first user option, (ii) the signal, and (iii) the second signal and to cause the vehicle controller to operate the electronically operated actuator and the second electronically operated actuator in a sixth manner based on (i) the second user option, (ii) the signal, and (ii) the second signal; and operating, with the vehicle controller, the second electronically operated actuator. 25. The method according to claim 24, wherein the second electronically operated actuator communicates with the vehicle controller via the first communication system. 26. The method according to claim 23, wherein the signal module and the vehicle controller operate on the same processor and the same memory. 27. The method according to claim 23, wherein the signal module and the vehicle controller operate on a different processor and a different memory. 28. A method of reconfiguring a vehicle control system in response to the addition of, or removal of, signal generator: determining, with a signal module comprising one or more processors coupled to memory, the presence of an added signal generator or removed signal generator; and implementing, with the signal module, an event handler in response to an added signal generator or removed signal generator; wherein the event handler: a) adds a user option storage configured to retain a value for a user option for the added signal generator and provides a control signal for a vehicle controller for the added signal generator if there is an added signal generator detected; or b) removes a user option storage configured to retain a value for a user option for the removed signal generator and removes a control signal for the vehicle controller for the removed signal generator if there is a removed signal generator detected, 

 thereby reconfiguring a vehicle control system in response to the addition of, or removal of, the signal generator. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein determining the presence of an added signal generator, comprises: Detecting, with the signal module, a signal from the added signal generator indicating that the added signal generator was added to the vehicle control system. 30. The method of claim 28, wherein determining the presence of removed signal generator, comprises: Detecting, with the signal module, an absence of signal from the removed signal generator indicating that the removed signal generator was removed from the vehicle control system.
